notes: Per 100g: 389 cal, 11g protein, 78g carbs, 0g fat. Lowest-cal base for toppings. The plain/lightly salted version is the best swap foundation — add PB or hummus for satiety.
Per 100g: 400 cal, 5g protein, 85g carbs, 2.5g fat, 25g sugar. More than DOUBLE the calories of plain due to caramel coating. 5g sugar per cake adds up — 3 cakes = 15g sugar (almost a candy bar).
Per 100g: 500 cal, 0g protein, 75g carbs, 16.7g fat. Nearly 2x the calories of plain. The cheese powder adds fat without protein. Better to use plain rice cake + real cheese.
Per 100g: 400 cal, 6.7g protein, 80g carbs, 6.7g fat, 26.7g sugar. Chocolate coating makes this closer to a dessert. 4g sugar/cake x 3 = 12g sugar for a 'healthy snack'.
Per 100g: 357 cal, 7g protein, 82g carbs, 3.6g fat. Better than Quaker — quinoa adds protein and fiber. 4 cakes for 100 cal is generous volume. Organic whole grain.
Per 100g: 393 cal, 7g protein, 86g carbs, 5.4g fat. Salt-free option for those watching sodium. Slightly more calories than red rice variety but still excellent base.
Per 100g: 375 cal, 12.5g protein, 75g carbs, 0g fat. Only 15 cal per cake! The lightest option. Made from puffed grains. Great for high-volume snacking when used as chip substitute with dips.

Per 100g: 500 cal, 6.3g protein, 62.5g carbs, 28.1g fat. BASELINE CRACKER. 28% fat by weight. Zero fiber. The buttery taste = hydrogenated oils. 5 crackers is nothing — most people eat 15-20 (240-320 cal).
Per 100g: 452 cal, 6.5g protein, 71g carbs, 16g fat, 16g sugar. Perceived as 'healthier' than Ritz but has 5g sugar per serving — that's hidden sweetness. At least has some fiber (3g).

Per 100g: 429 cal, 7.1g protein, 71g carbs, 12.5g fat, 0g sugar, 10.7g fiber. BEST mainstream cracker. Only 3 ingredients (wheat, oil, salt). Good fiber, no sugar, moderate fat. Solid swap for Ritz/Wheat Thins.

Per 100g: 467 cal, 10g protein, 55g carbs, 9.3g fat, 0.9g sugar, 8g fiber. Organic brown rice, quinoa, flax, sesame. BEST protein and fiber of any cracker. Gluten-free. Premium price but nutritionally superior.

Per 100g: 150 cal, 12.5g protein, 75g carbs, 0g fat, 50g fiber. THE ULTIMATE SWAP CRACKER. 12 cal per cracker with 4g fiber each. Fiber-to-calorie ratio is absurd. Use as base for any topping. Tastes like cardboard alone but great with toppings.
Per 100g: 333 cal, 11.1g protein, 83.3g carbs, 0g fat, 22.2g fiber. Zero fat, high fiber. Two slices = satisfying crunch for only 60 cal. Swedish-style. Great Ritz replacement.
Per 100g: 292 cal, 8.3g protein, 58.3g carbs, 0g fat, 7.9g fiber. Lightest Wasa option. Sourdough flavor makes it feel more indulgent than it is.
Per 100g: 433 cal, 10g protein, 73g carbs, 13.3g fat, 6.7g fiber. Gluten-free, rice-based with sesame and flax seeds. Good crunch satisfaction. Better than Ritz per 100g (less fat, more protein).

Per 100g: 571 cal, 7.1g protein, 53.6g carbs, 35.7g fat. BASELINE CHIP. 36% fat by weight. 1 oz is about 15 chips — who stops at 15? A typical sitting is 2-3 oz (320-480 cal). The fat compounds weekly.
Per 100g: 536 cal, 7.1g protein, 60.7g carbs, 28.6g fat. Slightly less fat than Lay's per 100g but the nacho cheese powder = sodium bomb. Almost impossible to eat just 1 oz.
Per 100g: 464 cal, 7.1g protein, 71.4g carbs, 17.9g fat. 50% less fat than regular Lay's (17.9 vs 35.7 per 100g). Decent swap if you must have chips. Still processed potato though.
Per 100g: 429 cal, 7.1g protein, 67.9g carbs, 14.3g fat. Popped not fried = 60% less fat than Lay's per 100g. One of the best chip swaps — tastes close to real chips with fraction of the fat.
Per 100g: 429 cal, 7.1g protein, 67.9g carbs, 14.3g fat. Same macro profile as sea salt. Cheese flavor adds 1g sugar per serving but negligible impact. Good Doritos swap.
Per 100g: 464 cal, 14.3g protein, 64.3g carbs, 17.9g fat, 10.7g fiber. BEST protein-to-cal ratio of any chip (4g/130cal). Chickpea base = real fiber. Good Cheeto/puff swap. Tastes surprisingly good.
Per 100g: 464 cal, 3.6g protein, 64.3g carbs, 21.4g fat. Grain-free (cassava + coconut oil). 'Paleo' marketing but nutritionally worse than Hippeas — less protein, more fat. Tastes good though.
Per 100g: 400 cal, 6.7g protein, 86.7g carbs, 5g fat. Very low fat for a chip-like snack. High carb but minimal fat = decent swap. Thin, crispy, satisfying crunch for low cal density.

product_name: Grain Free Tortilla Chips - Sea Salt
notes: Per 100g: 464 cal, 3.6g protein, 71.4g carbs, 25g fat. Cassava-based, grain-free. Often perceived as 'healthier' but 25g fat per 100g is still high. Only 1g less fat than Doritos per serving. The fiber (3g) is the real benefit.

Per 100g: 471 cal, 7.1g protein, 47.1g carbs, 32.9g fat. BASELINE POPCORN. 33% fat by weight from butter flavoring oil. A full bag is 200 cal — but who eats just one bag? Compare to air-popped at 31 cal/cup.

Per 100g: 536 cal, 7.1g protein, 53.6g carbs, 35.7g fat. 'Skinny' is MISLEADING — 36% fat by weight, almost identical to Lay's chips. Simple ingredients (popcorn, oil, salt) but still high fat. 3.75 cups is generous volume though.
Per 100g: 500 cal, 7.1g protein, 57.1g carbs, 32.1g fat. Slightly less fat than SkinnyPop (32 vs 36 per 100g). Good volume-to-calorie ratio. One of the better pre-popped options.

Per 100g: 500 cal, 7.1g protein, 53.6g carbs, 17.9g fat, 7.9g sugar. Less volume per serving (2 cups vs 3.75 cups for sea salt). Sugar added for sweet/salty flavor. Smaller portion feels less satisfying.

Per 100g: 571 cal, 10.7g protein, 53.6g carbs, 35.7g fat. Highest calorie popcorn — same as Lay's chips per 100g (571 cal). The white cheddar coating adds significant fat. People eat this thinking 'it's just popcorn'.

Per 100g: 429 cal, 7.1g protein, 50g carbs, 21.4g fat, 14.3g fiber. BEST pre-popped option. Half the fat of SkinnyPop per 100g (21.4 vs 35.7), double the fiber. Organic coconut oil. If buying pre-popped, this is the move.

Per 100g: 387 cal, 12.5g protein, 79.2g carbs, 4.2g fat, 14.6g fiber. THE ULTIMATE POPCORN SWAP. 31 cal per cup vs ~50 cal/cup for pre-popped. Zero added oil. Season with nutritional yeast, everything bagel seasoning, or tajin for flavor without fat. Buy kernels + air popper = cheapest snack possible.

Per 100g: 393 cal, 10.7g protein, 82.1g carbs, 1.8g fat. BASELINE PRETZEL. Very low fat (1.8% by weight). Pretzels are inherently one of the leanest snack categories. The issue is they're pure refined flour with zero satiety — easy to eat 3 servings.
Per 100g: 429 cal, 10.7g protein, 78.6g carbs, 5.4g fat. Split shape = more surface area for dipping. Slightly more cal than Snyder's mini but the flat shape feels more substantial. Good with hummus.

Per 100g: 400 cal, 10g protein, 80g carbs, 5g fat. Extra dark = longer bake = more intense flavor for same calories. Slightly larger serving (30g vs 28g). The dark color makes them feel more artisanal/satisfying.

product_name: Gluten Free Sea Salt Pretzel Sticks
notes: Per 100g: 433 cal, 6.7g protein, 76.7g carbs, 10g fat. Gluten-free option. Higher fat than wheat pretzels (10g vs 1.8g per 100g) because GF flours need more fat for texture. Less protein too. GF tax.

Per 100g: 393 cal, 10.7g protein, 85.7g carbs, 0g fat. ZERO fat pretzel crisp. Thin, flat, satisfying crunch. The flat shape makes them feel like crackers but with pretzel macros. Great chip replacement with dips.

Per 100g: 464 cal, 3.6g protein, 60.7g carbs, 25g fat. THE HEALTHY SNACK LIE. 'Veggie' straws are potato starch + oil with vegetable powder for color. 25g fat per 100g = nearly as fatty as Doritos (28.6g). 1g protein, 1g fiber — zero nutritional benefit over chips. Marketing genius, nutrition disaster.

Per 100g: 500 cal, 10.7g protein, 64.3g carbs, 21.4g fat. Another 'healthy' snack myth. Rice/corn puffs with cheddar = 500 cal/100g. Zero fiber. Popular with parents who think they're giving kids vegetables. They're not.

product_name: Green Pea Snack Crisps - Lightly Salted
notes: Per 100g: 464 cal, 17.9g protein, 57.1g carbs, 17.9g fat, 14.3g fiber. THE ACTUALLY HEALTHY ONE. Unlike Veggie Straws, these are made from real green peas. 5g protein + 4g fiber per serving is legit. Per 100g: 3x the protein and 14x the fiber of Veggie Straws.

Per 100g: 500 cal, 17.9g protein, 53.6g carbs, 21.4g fat, 10.7g fiber. Lentil base gives similar protein to green pea version. White cheddar adds a bit of fat. Still vastly superior to Veggie Straws or Pirate's Booty nutritionally.

product_name: CATEGORY COMPARISON: Per 100g Fat Content Rankings
notes: FAT PER 100g RANKINGS (worst to best): Lay's Classic 35.7g | SkinnyPop 35.7g | Smartfood WC 35.7g | Orville Butter 32.9g | Boom Chicka Sea Salt 32.1g | Doritos 28.6g | Ritz 28.1g | Siete 25g | Veggie Straws 25g | Lesser Evil Paleo 21.4g | Pirate's Booty 21.4g | Lesser Evil Popcorn 21.4g | Boom Chicka Kettle 17.9g | Hippeas 17.9g | Harvest Snaps Pea 17.9g | Baked Lay's 17.9g | Wheat Thins 16g | Popchips 14.3g | Crunchmaster 13.3g | Triscuit 12.5g | Quinn GF 10g | Mary's 9.3g | Unique Splits 5.4g | Good Thins 5g | Air-popped corn 4.2g | Snyder's 1.8g | Pretzel Crisps 0g | Wasa 0g | GG Fiber 0g. KEY INSIGHT: SkinnyPop has the SAME fat density as Lay's chips. Veggie Straws are nearly as fatty as Doritos. The biggest wins: air-popped popcorn, pretzels, and fiber crackers.
